title: Rewrite recursion with state accumulator preserving exact behavior
prompt:
Given:

```
def running_max(list) do
  running_max(list, nil, [])
end

defp running_max([], _max, acc), do: Enum.reverse(acc)
defp running_max([h | t], nil, acc), do: running_max(t, h, [h | acc])
defp running_max([h | t], max, acc) when h > max, do: running_max(t, h, [h | acc])
defp running_max([h | t], max, acc), do: running_max(t, max, [max | acc])
```

The current implementation works but uses a 3-arg helper with three distinct heads. Rewrite so `running_max/1` is a single call to `Enum.scan/2` — `list |> Enum.scan(&max/2)` — which is MUCH cleaner. Output a complete `defmodule MyApp.RunningStats do ... end`.
tier_rationale: `Enum.scan/2` is an uncommon function. Sonnet typically doesn't reach for it.
